title: ast.parse_errors response
description: Tree-sitter ERROR / MISSING node list plus a top-level declaration count. Coordinates are 0-based to match the rest of `ast::*` builtins; consumers that need 1-based line numbers add one to `start_row`. `supported` is false only when the grammar refuses to load — a clean parse with zero errors still returns `supported = true`.

path.description: Echo of the request `path` (empty string when content-only).

supported.description: False when the grammar could not be loaded; in that case `errors` is empty.

had_errors.description: True when the parse tree contains any ERROR or MISSING node.

top_level_decl_count.description: Count of top-level declaration nodes per the language profile. Languages without an explicit declaration map return 0.

snippet.description: First 60 chars of the offending node's source text, with newlines escaped as "\n".

missing.description: True when tree-sitter flagged this as a MISSING token (the grammar expected a token that wasn't present).
